# dynamodb-toolkit — Full Reference (v3.7.0)

> Zero-runtime-dependency, ESM-only micro-library for AWS DynamoDB built on the AWS JS SDK v3 (`@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb` + `@aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb`). Provides the `Adapter` class, expression builders, batch + transaction chunking, resumable mass operations, cascade primitives, optimistic concurrency, scope-freeze, marshallers, a framework-agnostic REST core, a `node:http` handler, and table-provisioning helpers + CLI.

This file is the long-form companion to `llms.txt`. Read `llms.txt` first for the API surface; this file documents semantics, edge cases, and the per-module helper API.

## 1. Adapter

`Adapter` (`src/adapter/adapter.js`) is the composition root. One Adapter typically maps to one logical entity (usually one table, though multi-type shared tables work via `typeDiscriminator`/`typeLabels`). It owns long-lived state (`client`, `table`, `keyFields`, hooks) and delegates real work to the `expressions/`, `batch/`, `mass/`, and `paths/` modules — all independently importable.

### 1.1 Constructor

```js
import {DynamoDBClient} from '@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb';
import {DynamoDBDocumentClient} from '@aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb';
import {Adapter} from 'dynamodb-toolkit';

const client = DynamoDBDocumentClient.from(new DynamoDBClient({region: 'us-east-1'}), {
  marshallOptions: {removeUndefinedValues: true}
});

const adapter = new Adapter({
  client,                           // required
  table: 'rentals',                 // required
  keyFields: [                      // required; partition key first, optional sort second
    'state',
    {name: 'rentalId', type: 'number', width: 5},
    'carVin'
  ],
  structuralKey: {name: '-sk', separator: '|'},   // required when keyFields.length > 1
  technicalPrefix: '-',                           // opt-in adapter-managed-field prefix
  typeLabels: ['state', 'rental', 'car'],         // paired 1:1 with keyFields
  typeDiscriminator: {name: 'kind'},
  typeField: 'kind',                              // auto-populate via built-in prepare
  indices: {
    'by-status-date': {
      type: 'gsi',
      pk: 'status',
      sk: {name: 'createdAt', type: 'number'},
      projection: 'all',
      sparse: true
    },
    'by-name': {type: 'lsi', sk: 'name', projection: 'all'}
  },
  filterable: {
    status: ['eq', 'in'],
    year: {ops: ['eq', 'ge', 'le', 'btw'], type: 'number'}
  },
  searchable: {name: 1},
  versionField: '-version',
  createdAtField: '-createdAt',
  relationships: {structural: true},
  descriptorKey: '__adapter__',
  hooks: {
    prepare: stampCreatedAtISO('-createdAt'),
    validateItem: async item => { if (!item.name) throw new Error('name is required'); }
  }
});
```

`client`, `table`, and a non-empty `keyFields` are required; the constructor throws synchronously without them.

**Normalization performed at construction:**
- `keyFields` → `Required<KeyFieldSpec>[]` with `{name, type, width?}` per entry. Bare strings become `{name, type: 'string'}`. `{type: 'number'}` in a composite requires `width`.
- `structuralKey` → `{name, separator}` with `separator` defaulting to `'|'`.
- `indices` entries — `pk`/`sk` bare strings → `{name, type: 'string'}`; missing `projection` → `'all'`; missing `sparse` → `false`; missing `indirect` → `false`. Legacy `indirectIndices` entries are synthesised into `indices` as `{type: 'gsi', indirect: true, projection: 'keys-only'}`.
- `typeDiscriminator` bare string → `{name}`.
- `technicalPrefix` validates that every declared adapter-managed field (structural key, version, createdAt, searchable mirror prefix, descriptor key) starts with the prefix.
- `hooks` are merged with `defaultHooks` and composed with the built-in prepare/revive steps described below.
- `relationships.structural` requires composite `keyFields` + `structuralKey`.
- `typeLabels` prefix-collision check: no label may be a prefix of another — needed for `{self: true}` correctness in `buildKey`.

**Derived fields on the instance:**
- `primaryKeyAttrs: string[]` — DB primary-key attribute names. `[keyFields[0].name, structuralKey.name]` for composite, `[keyFields[0].name]` for single.
- `keysOnlyFields: string[]` — key attributes for `options.keysOnly` / `?fields=*keys`.
- `filterableTypes: Record<string, 'string' | 'number' | 'binary'>` — per-field coercion types inferred from `filterable` + `keyFields` + `indices`.

### 1.2 Built-in prepare / revive steps

When `technicalPrefix` is declared, the constructor installs a built-in prepare step that runs **before** the user's `prepare` hook. On every non-patch write it:

1. Validates no user field starts with `technicalPrefix`.
2. Composes the **structural key** (contiguous-from-start `keyFields` joined by `separator`, zero-padded per `width` on `{type: 'number'}` components).
3. Writes `searchable` mirror columns (`searchablePrefix + field` → lowercased value).
4. Writes sparse-index marker fields per each `indices[*].sparse` predicate.
5. Writes `typeField = typeOf(item)` when `typeField` is declared and the field isn't already set by the user.
6. Inits `versionField` to `1` when declared and the item doesn't carry one (post-only).

On patches, only #1 + the partial structural-key recomputation (when the patch touches a keyField component) run; mirrors/markers/typeField are left to the user if they need partial-update reshaping.

The built-in revive step strips every field starting with `technicalPrefix` — except `versionField` and `createdAtField`, which round-trip through the client so callers can do read-modify-write cycles without re-sending. User `revive` hooks run last.

### 1.3 The `Raw<T>` bypass marker

`Raw<T>` (single class — the v2 `Raw` / `DbRaw` pair was collapsed) wraps an item to signal "I already shaped this for the DB; skip `prepare` and `validateItem`."

```js
import {raw, Raw} from 'dynamodb-toolkit';

await adapter.put(raw({name: 'X', '-internal': 'kept'}), {force: true});
const item = await adapter.getByKey({name: 'X'}, undefined, {reviveItems: false});
// → instanceof Raw; item.item is the verbatim raw record
```

Marshalling (Set / Buffer handling) still runs unconditionally — `Raw` only affects the toolkit's hook layer, not the SDK middleware. `Raw` bypasses the built-in prepare step too: callers are responsible for correct structural-key formation and technical-field stamping.

### 1.4 Hooks

| Hook | Default | When called |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `prepare(item, isPatch?)` | identity (chained after built-in) | Before every non-`Raw` write. Built-in step runs first (see §1.2). |
| `prepareKey(key, index?)` | identity | Before every key-only operation; restricted to `keyFields` / index schema afterwards. |
| `prepareListInput(example, index?)` | `() => ({})` | Supplies `IndexName` + `KeyConditionExpression` for list ops. Shallow-merged into params before caller's paging/filter/projection. |
| `updateInput(input, op)` | identity | Last-chance hook to mutate the SDK Command input before dispatch. `op.name` ∈ `'post' \| 'put' \| 'patch' \| 'delete'`; `op.force` set for forced `put`. Runs after `buildUpdate` / `buildCondition` / existence checks but before `cleanParams`. |
| `revive(rawItem, fields?)` | `subsetObject(rawItem, fields)` | After every read. Built-in step (strip `technicalPrefix` keys, preserve `versionField` / `createdAtField`) runs first. |
| `validateItem(item, isPatch?)` | `async () => {}` | Async validator; throw to abort the write. Not called on `Raw`-wrapped items. |
| `checkConsistency(batch)` | `async () => null` | Returns extra `make*` descriptors (or `[]`) to bundle into the same `TransactWriteItems`. `null` takes the single-Command fast path. |

Hooks live in `this.hooks`. User overrides via `options.hooks` merge over the defaults; subclass overrides on `this.hooks` work too. Subclassing the Adapter and overriding methods is also supported — internal methods prefixed with `_` are call sites designed for override.

### 1.5 CRUD reads

- `await adapter.getByKey(key, fields?, options?)` — options: `{consistent?, reviveItems?, ignoreIndirection?, params?}`. Returns `TItem | undefined`. Indirect-index hits trigger a second-hop `GetItem` against the base table.
- `await adapter.getByKeys(keys, fields?, options?)` — length-preserving; `result[i]` is the revived item for `keys[i]` or `undefined` on miss. Callers filter `Boolean` if they want compact results. `BatchGetItem` with `UnprocessedKeys` retry.
- `await adapter.getList(options?, example?, index?)` — `example` fed to `prepareListInput`. Returns `{data, offset, limit, total?}`. `options.sort` auto-resolves to an index via `findIndexForSort` unless an explicit `index` argument is passed.
- `await adapter.getListByParams(params, options?)` — skips `prepareListInput`; `params` is the prepared Query/Scan input.
- `await adapter.getListUnder(partialKey, options?)` — sugar for `getListByParams(buildKey(partialKey), options)`. Children-only descendants under `partialKey`. For `{self}` / `{partial}` shapes, compose `buildKey` + `getListByParams` directly.

**`ListOptions`:**

```ts
interface ListOptions {
  offset?: number;             // default 0
  limit?: number;              // default 10
  descending?: boolean;        // reverses ScanIndexForward
  sort?: string;               // auto-resolved via findIndexForSort; throws NoIndexForSortField
  useIndex?: string;           // explicit index override; bypasses sort inference
  consistent?: boolean;
  fields?: string | string[] | null;
  keysOnly?: boolean;          // shortcut for projecting adapter.keysOnlyFields
  search?: string;             // free-form substring; requires searchable declared
  prefix?: string;             // searchable-prefix override (default adapter.searchablePrefix)
  caseSensitive?: boolean;     // for search
  filter?: FilterClause[];     // structured filter grammar (see §6.1, §6.5)
  needTotal?: boolean;         // default true; false skips COUNT pass
  reviveItems?: boolean;       // false → Raw<T>
  ignoreIndirection?: boolean;
  includeDescriptor?: boolean; // true → include the reserved descriptor record
}
```

`needTotal: false` skips the `Select: 'COUNT'` pagination. `paginateList` always accumulates matches when a `FilterExpression` is set (DynamoDB's `Limit` is pre-filter, so naive use returns short pages).

### 1.6 CRUD writes — single

Every single write funnels through `dispatchWrite(client, batch, checks)` (`src/adapter/transaction-upgrade.js`): build → `checkConsistency` → single Command or `TransactWriteItems`.

- `await adapter.post(item, {returnFailedItem?}?)` — create-only (`attribute_not_exists(<pk>)`). Throws `ConditionalCheckFailedException` on collision; with `returnFailedItem`, the error carries the colliding item on `.Item`.
- `await adapter.put(item, {force?, conditions?, params?, returnFailedItem?}?)` — default conditions on `attribute_exists(<pk>)`; `force` skips. With `versionField`, conditions on `attribute_not_exists(<pk>) OR <versionField> = :observed`, writes `observed + 1` (or `1` when the item is new). `force: true` skips the OC check but still bumps the version.
- `await adapter.patch(key, patch, options?)` — options: `{delete?, separator?, arrayOps?, conditions?, expectedVersion?, params?, returnFailedItem?}`. Key fields auto-stripped from `patch`. With `versionField`: default conditions on `attribute_exists(<pk>)` when `expectedVersion` is omitted; conditions on `<versionField> = :expectedVersion` when supplied. UpdateExpression always `ADD <versionField> :1` when the field is declared.
- `await adapter.edit(key, mapFn, options?)` — read → mapFn(revived) → shallow diff → `UpdateItem`. Returns the revived new item on success; `undefined` when the source is missing or `mapFn` returns falsy. Options: `{readFields?, conditions?, allowKeyChange?, params?, returnFailedItem?}`. Throws `KeyFieldChanged` when the diff touches a keyField without `allowKeyChange: true`; with the flag, auto-promotes to a `move` transaction. Unchanged items short-circuit (no WCU spent).
- `await adapter.delete(key, options?)` — options: `{conditions?, expectedVersion?, params?, returnFailedItem?}`. Idempotent without conditions. With `versionField` + `expectedVersion`, conditions on `<versionField> = :expectedVersion`.
- `await adapter.clone(key, mapFn?, options?)` — read → mapFn → post (or `put({force})` with `options.force`). Returns the cloned item or `undefined` on source miss. Options: `{force?, params?, reviveItems?, ignoreIndirection?}`.
- `await adapter.move(key, mapFn?, options?)` — read → mapFn → put-dst + delete-src bundled into a `TransactWriteItems`. Returns the moved item or `undefined`. Same options as `clone`.

**`returnFailedItem: true`** sets `ReturnValuesOnConditionCheckFailure: 'ALL_OLD'` on the underlying request. Thrown `ConditionalCheckFailedException.Item` carries the colliding item. Inside auto-upgraded transactions, the matching `TransactionCanceledException.CancellationReasons[i].Item` is populated — pair with `explainTransactionCancellation` (§3) to surface the failing descriptor + pre-existing item in one call.

### 1.7 CRUD writes — mass

All mass ops carry a uniform options bag; the bulk-individual variants (`putItems`, `deleteByKeys`, `cloneByKeys`, `moveByKeys`) return `{processed: number}`; the list-op variants return `MassOpResult` (§4.1).

**`MassOptions`:**

```ts
interface MassOptions {
  strategy?: 'native' | 'sequential'; // default 'native' (BatchWriteItem); sequential = per-item Commands
  params?: Record<string, unknown>;
  maxItems?: number;                  // page-boundary soft cap; resumable list-ops only
  resumeToken?: string;               // from prior MassOpResult.cursor
  ifNotExists?: boolean;              // per-item attribute_not_exists (routes to PutCommand path)
  ifExists?: boolean;                 // per-item attribute_exists (mutually exclusive with ifNotExists)
  asOf?: Date | string | number;      // scope-freeze; requires createdAtField declared
  includeDescriptor?: boolean;        // include reserved descriptor row in the scan
}
```

Methods:
- `putItems(items, options?)` → `{processed}`
- `deleteByKeys(keys, options?)` → `{processed}`
- `deleteListByParams(params, options?)` → `MassOpResult` (resumable)
- `cloneByKeys(keys, mapFn, options?)` → `{processed}`
- `cloneListByParams(params, mapFn, options?)` → `MassOpResult` (resumable; `mapFn` returning falsy drops that item silently)
- `moveByKeys(keys, mapFn, options?)` → `{processed}` (sum of put + delete = ~2× item count on success)
- `moveListByParams(params, mapFn, options?)` → `MassOpResult`
- `editListByParams(params, mapFn, options?)` → `MassOpResult` — per-item read → diff → UpdateItem. Unchanged items → `skipped`; `mapFn` returning falsy → `skipped`; items that vanish mid-scan → `skipped` (via CCF on the existence guard). Key-touching diffs → `failed` (or auto-promote to move with `allowKeyChange`).
- `rename(fromExample, toExample, options?)` → `MassOpResult` — subtree rename, constructive-before-destructive. Put at destination then delete at source. `options.kind` ∈ `'exact' \| 'children'` (default `'children'`). Crashes mid-phase leave source intact; resume completes delete. Put collisions → `skipped`. `options.mapFn` composes after the `swapPrefix(fromExample, toExample)` mapping. Non-transactional — caller's job to coordinate reads during the rename window.
- `cloneWithOverwrite(fromExample, toExample, options?)` → `MassOpResult` — subtree clone, destructive-before-constructive. Delete at destination then put. Source remains intact. Caller asserts destination is disposable.

**Cursor semantics:** `cursor` is present iff the op stopped early (`maxItems` reached). Resumption is page-boundary, so the cursor is safe across versions within the same workstream. Cursor is base64-url JSON — opaque to callers; `decodeCursor` is a debug helper, not a stable public API.

**Idempotency:** re-running a mass op with the same `params` + `resumeToken` is safe:
- `deleteListByParams` — re-delete of already-absent items succeeds (DynamoDB Delete is idempotent).
- `cloneListByParams` / `moveListByParams` — put-collisions during resume bucket into `skipped` if `ifNotExists` is set; otherwise clobber (default BatchWrite semantics).

### 1.8 Cascade primitives (A6')

Gated by `relationships.structural` on the adapter. Throws `CascadeNotDeclared` when called without the declaration.

- `deleteAllUnder(srcKey, options?)` → `MassOpResult` — leaf-first. Delete every descendant of `srcKey` via `deleteListByParams(buildKey(srcKey))`, then the self node. Self-delete runs only when descendants pagination completes (no intermediate cursor).
- `cloneAllUnder(srcKey, dstKey, options?)` / `cloneAllUnderBy(srcKey, mapFn, options?)` — root-first. Copy self + descendants. The prefix-swap variant uses `swapPrefix(srcKey, dstKey)` + `options.mapFn` composition; the `-By` variant delegates destination choice to `mapFn`. Source untouched.
- `moveAllUnder(srcKey, dstKey, options?)` / `moveAllUnderBy(srcKey, mapFn, options?)` — leaf-first, two-phase idempotent (shared `_subtreeRename` with `rename`).

All cascade primitives honour `options.maxItems` / `options.resumeToken`. The self-node phase runs only on the first invocation (cloneAll/moveAll root-first) or the final invocation (deleteAll leaf-first).

### 1.9 Batch builders

Each `make*` returns a discriminated descriptor `{action, params}` (plus `adapter` for `makeGet`). Compose with `applyTransaction` / `applyBatch` / `getBatch` / `getTransaction`.

- `await adapter.makeGet(key, fields?, params?)` → `{action: 'get', adapter, params}` — back-reference lets result routers revive against the right Adapter in multi-table reads.
- `await adapter.makeCheck(key, params?)` → `{action: 'check', params}` — condition-only, for `applyTransaction`.
- `await adapter.makePost(item, {returnFailedItem?}?)` → `{action: 'put', params}` with `attribute_not_exists(<pk>)`.
- `await adapter.makePut(item, {force?, conditions?, expectedVersion?, params?, returnFailedItem?}?)` → `{action: 'put', params}`.
- `await adapter.makePatch(key, patch, options?)` → `{action: 'patch', params}` with the built `UpdateExpression`.
- `await adapter.makeDelete(key, {conditions?, expectedVersion?, params?, returnFailedItem?}?)` → `{action: 'delete', params}`.

### 1.10 Query helpers

- `adapter.typeOf(item)` → `string | number | undefined`. Priority: `typeDiscriminator.name` value on the item (coerced to string) → `typeLabels[depth-1]` where `depth` = count of contiguous-from-start defined `keyFields` → raw `depth` number → undefined.
- `adapter.findIndexForSort(field)` → index name. Scans `this.indices` for entries whose `sk.name` matches `field`. Prefers LSI over GSI when both match. Throws `NoIndexForSortField` — the toolkit does not in-memory-sort.
- `adapter.applyFilter(params, clauses)` → mutates `params`. Validates against `filterable`, coerces values per `filterableTypes`, auto-promotes index-compatible clauses to `KeyConditionExpression`, the rest land in `FilterExpression`. Throws `BadFilterField` / `BadFilterOp`.
- `adapter.buildKey(values, options?, params?)` → mutates `params`. Keys against the main table (default) or a declared index (`options.indexName`). Default: children-only `begins_with` on the structural key. `{self: true}` → self + descendants (`begins_with` without the trailing separator; requires `typeLabels` to be non-prefix-colliding). `{partial: 'abc'}` → narrows next tier to items starting with `abc`. Single-field `keyFields` emit pk equality; `self`/`partial` rejected. Values must be contiguous-from-start.
- `adapter.swapPrefix(srcPrefix, dstPrefix)` → `mapFn` that rewrites the leading prefix. Throws at construction on prefix-shape invariant violations; throws at apply time when an item's values don't match `srcPrefix` (signals a mis-scoped upstream query).
- `adapter.overlayFields(overlay)` → `mapFn` that merges `overlay` into each item. Validates that overlay doesn't null/undefined a keyField.

### 1.11 Transaction auto-upgrade

Every write CRUD method funnels through `dispatchWrite(client, batch, checks)` (`src/adapter/transaction-upgrade.js`):

1. Build the batch descriptor via `makePost` / `makePut` / `makePatch` / `makeDelete`.
2. Call `hooks.checkConsistency(descriptor)`.
3. If the result is `null` → dispatch as a single `PutCommand` / `UpdateCommand` / `DeleteCommand` (fast path).
4. If the result is any array (even `[]`) → bundle main + checks into a `TransactWriteCommand`.
5. Throw `TransactionLimitExceededError` when total > `TRANSACTION_LIMIT` (100).

### 1.12 Indirect indices

Mark sparse GSIs that project keys only:

```js
new Adapter({
  client, table, keyFields: ['name'],
  indices: {
    'climate-index': {type: 'gsi', pk: 'climate', projection: 'keys-only', indirect: true}
  }
});
```

`getByKey({climate: 'frozen'}, undefined, {params: {IndexName: 'climate-index'}})` does:

1. `GetItem` against the GSI → extracts the base-table key.
2. `GetItem` against the base table → full record.

`getByKeys` / `getList` / `getListByParams` also second-hop via `readByKeys`. Pass `{ignoreIndirection: true}` to skip.

Legacy `indirectIndices: {'name-of-gsi': 1}` is still accepted — normalised into `indices['name-of-gsi'] = {type: 'gsi', indirect: true, projection: 'keys-only'}`.

### 1.13 Deprecated aliases (still work with `warnOnce`)

- `getAll` / `getAllByParams` → `getList` / `getListByParams`
- `putAll` → `putItems`
- `deleteAllByParams` → `deleteListByParams`
- `cloneAllByParams` → `cloneListByParams`
- `moveAllByParams` → `moveListByParams`

First call emits `console.warn`; subsequent calls are silent. Planned removal in a future minor or 4.0.

## 2. Expressions (`dynamodb-toolkit/expressions`)

Pure functions. Each accepts a `params` object and returns a new params with the relevant expression strings + attribute maps populated. All handle name de-duplication, alias namespacing (each builder has its own placeholder prefix: `#upk`/`:upv` for update, `#kc`/`:kcv` for key-condition, `#cd`/`:cdv` for condition, `#sr`/`:flt` for search, `:fbe` for filter-by-example, `#pj` for projection, `#ff`/`:ffv` for Adapter's applyFilter), and reuse of pre-existing `ExpressionAttributeNames` / `ExpressionAttributeValues`.

### 2.1 `buildUpdate(patch, options?, params?)`

Builds `UpdateExpression`. Mutates and returns `params`.

- `options.delete: string[]` → `REMOVE` clauses.
- `options.separator` (default `'.'`) splits dotted paths. Pure-digit segments become array indices.
- `options.arrayOps: ArrayOp[]` — atomic array / Set operations:

| `op` | DDB expression | Required fields |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `append` | `SET path = list_append(if_not_exists(path, :empty), :values)` | `path`, `values` |
| `prepend` | `SET path = list_append(:values, if_not_exists(path, :empty))` | `path`, `values` |
| `setAtIndex` | `SET path[i] = :value` | `path`, `index`, `value` |
| `removeAtIndex` | `REMOVE path[i]` | `path`, `index` (leaves hole — DDB doesn't shift) |
| `add` | `ADD path :value` | `path`, `value` (atomic increment / Set add) |

### 2.2 `addProjection(params, fields, projectionFieldMap?, skipSelect?, separator?)`

Builds `ProjectionExpression`. De-duplicates, applies `projectionFieldMap` aliases to the first path segment. Sets `Select: 'SPECIFIC_ATTRIBUTES'` unless `skipSelect`.

### 2.3 `buildSearch(searchable, query, options?, params?)`

Builds a substring `FilterExpression` over `searchablePrefix + field` mirror columns. Default: case-insensitive. Options: `{fields?, prefix?, caseSensitive?}`.

### 2.4 `buildFilterByExample(example, params?)`

Builds an equality `FilterExpression` from a partial object: `{climate: 'frozen', gravity: '1 standard'}` → `#fbe0 = :fbe0 AND #fbe1 = :fbe1`.

### 2.5 `buildCondition(clauses, params?)`

Declarative `ConditionExpression` builder. Clause shapes:

```ts
type ConditionClause =
  | {path: string; op: '=' | '<>' | '<' | '<=' | '>' | '>='; value: unknown}
  | {path: string; op: 'exists' | 'notExists'}
  | {path: string; op: 'beginsWith' | 'contains'; value: unknown}
  | {path: string; op: 'in'; values: unknown[]}
  | {op: 'and' | 'or'; clauses: ConditionClause[]}
  | {op: 'not'; clause: ConditionClause};
```

### 2.6 `buildKeyCondition(input, params?)`

Low-level primitive for `KeyConditionExpression`. Caller supplies a fully-prepared value string; for the ergonomic surface that builds the prefix from `keyFields` / `structuralKey` automatically, use `adapter.buildKey(values, options, params)` instead.

```ts
interface KeyConditionInput {
  name: string;          // sort/structural key field name (e.g. '-sk')
  value: string;         // fully-prepared value; caller joins keyFields components
  kind: 'exact' | 'prefix';  // = or begins_with
  pkName?: string;       // partition-key field (optional — omit for a bare sk-only clause)
  pkValue?: unknown;     // partition-key value (required when pkName is set)
}
```

### 2.7 `cleanParams(params)` / `cloneParams(params)`

- `cleanParams(params)` drops unused entries from `ExpressionAttributeNames` / `ExpressionAttributeValues` (anything no expression actually references). Regex-based; relies on `\b` word boundaries + numeric-suffix naming convention.
- `cloneParams(params)` shallow-clones `params` with deep-cloned attribute maps. Use before mutating when the caller may reuse `params`.

## 3. Batch (`dynamodb-toolkit/batch`)

All accept `(client, ...descriptors)` where descriptors are `{action, params}` items or arrays of items (arrays are flattened; `null`/`undefined` entries skipped).

- `applyBatch(client, ...descriptors)` → `Promise<number>` — chunked `BatchWriteItem` (25 per call), `UnprocessedItems` retry with exponential jitter.
- `applyTransaction(client, ...descriptors)` → `Promise<number>` — single `TransactWriteItems` call. Throws if total > `TRANSACTION_LIMIT` (100). Accepts `{options: {clientRequestToken?, returnConsumedCapacity?, returnItemCollectionMetrics?}}` sentinel at any position for transaction-level knobs; multiple `{options}` entries merge, later fields winning.
- `explainTransactionCancellation(err, ...descriptors)` → `{failures, message} | null` — maps `TransactionCanceledException.CancellationReasons` back to input descriptors; returns `null` for non-cancellation errors so callers can early-exit in `catch`. `failures[i]` has `{index, descriptor, code, message?, item?}`; `item` is populated when the matching descriptor set `returnFailedItem: true`. Walks args identically to `applyTransaction` (null skipped, arrays flattened, `{options}` sentinels ignored).
- `getBatch(client, ...descriptors)` → `Promise<{table, item, adapter?}[]>` — chunked `BatchGetItem` (100 per call), `UnprocessedKeys` retry. `adapter` present when the descriptor came from `makeGet`.
- `getTransaction(client, ...descriptors)` → `Promise<{table, item, adapter?}[]>` — single `TransactGetItems`.
- `backoff(from = 50, to = 20_000, finite?)` — generator yielding exponential delays with full jitter. Default AWS "stop around one minute" shape (8 attempts with `from: 50`, `to: 20_000`).
- `TRANSACTION_LIMIT` — `100`.

## 4. Mass ops (`dynamodb-toolkit/mass`)

Three categories:

1. **Individual ops** on single items live on the Adapter (see §1.6).
2. **Bulk-individual ops** take N caller-supplied items or keys and batch them. The caller defines the set; the helper returns a result aligned to that set: `readByKeys`, `writeItems`, `deleteByKeys`.
3. **List ops** use `Query` / `Scan` — the database produces the set. Read-shaped: `readList`, `readListGetItems`, `iterateList`, `iterateItems`, `paginateList`, `getTotal`. Consume-shaped: `deleteList`, `copyList`, `moveList` — walk pages and apply per-item ops.

Naming follows the categories: "List" in a helper name means DB-produces-set; bulk-individual helpers drop "List" because the caller supplies the items.

### 4.1 Cursor + envelope

```ts
interface MassOpOptions {
  ifNotExists?: boolean;         // per-item attribute_not_exists (routes to PutCommand path)
  ifExists?: boolean;            // per-item attribute_exists
  maxItems?: number;             // soft cap (page-boundary)
  resumeToken?: string;          // from prior MassOpResult.cursor
}

interface MassOpFailure {
  key: Record<string, unknown>;
  reason: 'ConditionalCheckFailed' | 'ValidationException' | 'ProvisionedThroughputExceeded' | 'Unknown';
  details?: string;
  sdkError?: unknown;
}

interface MassOpConflict {
  key: Record<string, unknown>;
  reason: 'VersionConflict';
  sdkError?: unknown;            // populated when versionField declared
}

interface MassOpResult {
  processed: number;
  skipped: number;
  failed: MassOpFailure[];
  conflicts: MassOpConflict[];
  cursor?: string;               // present iff stopped early
}
```

- `encodeCursor(payload)` / `decodeCursor(cursor)` — base64url JSON. `decodeCursor` is debug/test only; callers round-trip cursors opaquely.
- `runPaged(client, params, onPage, options?)` — low-level paged runner underpinning `deleteListByParams` / `cloneListByParams` / `moveListByParams` / `editListByParams` / `cascadeAll*`.

### 4.2 List primitives

- `paginateList(client, params, options?, needTotal?, minLimit?, maxLimit?)` → `PaginatedResult`. Accumulates matches when `FilterExpression` is set. `minLimit`/`maxLimit` default `10` / `100`.
- `iterateList(client, params)` — async generator yielding raw page objects.
- `iterateItems(client, params)` — async generator yielding individual items.
- `readList(client, params, fn)` — call `fn(page)` once, return `nextParams | null`.
- `readListGetItems(client, params)` → `{nextParams, items}` — single page.
- `getTotal(client, params)` → `Promise<number>` — `Select: 'COUNT'` pagination.

### 4.3 Bulk-individual primitives

- `readByKeys(client, table, keys, params?)` → `(Item | undefined)[]` — length-preserving; `BatchGetItem` + `UnprocessedKeys` retry.
- `writeItems(client, table, items, mapFn?)` → `Promise<number>` — `BatchWriteItem` puts, `UnprocessedItems` retry.
- `deleteByKeys(client, table, keys)` → `Promise<number>` — `BatchWriteItem` deletes.

### 4.4 Consume-shaped

- `deleteList(client, params, keyFn?)` → `Promise<number>` — read-then-batch-delete.
- `copyList(client, params, mapFn?)` → `Promise<number>`.
- `moveList(client, params, mapFn?, keyFn?)` → `Promise<number>` — paired 12 puts + 12 deletes per `BatchWrite` chunk (24 ≤ 25 limit).

### 4.5 Map-fn utilities

- `mergeMapFn(...fns)` — left-to-right composition of per-item transforms. `mergeMapFn(a, b)(item)` returns `b(a(item))`. Falsy intermediate results short-circuit.

### 4.6 Deprecated aliases

Available with `warnOnce` deprecation — removed in a future minor.

- `readListByKeys` / `readOrderedListByKeys` → `readByKeys`
- `deleteListByKeys` → `deleteByKeys`
- `writeList` → `writeItems`

## 5. Paths (`dynamodb-toolkit/paths`)

Pure path utilities over plain JS objects — no SDK involved.

- `getPath(obj, path, defaultValue?, separator?)`
- `setPath(obj, path, value, separator?)` — mutates `obj`, returns it; safeguards against `__proto__` / prototype-chain writes.
- `deletePath(obj, path, separator?)` — same prototype safety.
- `applyPatch(obj, patch, options?)` — applies a patch with `delete` / `separator` / `arrayOps` semantics in memory.
- `normalizeFields(fields, projectionFieldMap?, separator?)` — accepts string, comma-separated string, array, or object; returns `string[] | null`. `null` means "no projection constraint."
- `subsetObject(obj, fields, separator?)` — picks fields at paths, returns a new object.

Pure-digit segments are array indices.

## 6. REST core (`dynamodb-toolkit/rest-core`)

Framework-agnostic helpers. Each parser accepts the raw query value (string or array) and returns a normalized shape. Each builder accepts a result/policy and produces a wire-shaped object.

### 6.1 Parsers

- `parseFields(input, {maxItems?})` → `string[] | null`. `maxItems` default `1000`; extra segments truncated (DoS guard). `*keys` wildcard signals "just the key fields" and is recognised by `buildListOptions` → `keysOnly: true`.
- `parseSort(input)` → `{field, direction: 'asc' | 'desc', chain?: SortClause[]} | null` — leading `-` flips direction.
- `parseFilter(query)` → `FilterClause[]` — walks every `<op>-<field>=<value>` entry in the query map (op prefix in the allowed set: `eq ne lt le gt ge in btw beg ct ex nx`). Unrecognised op prefixes are skipped (treated as ordinary query params). Shape is polymorphic by op:
  ```ts
  type FilterClause =
    | {field: string; op: 'eq' | 'ne' | 'lt' | 'le' | 'gt' | 'ge' | 'beg' | 'ct'; value: string}
    | {field: string; op: 'in' | 'btw'; value: string[]}
    | {field: string; op: 'ex' | 'nx'};
  ```
  Multi-value ops (`in` / `btw`) use **first-character delimiter**: `?in-status=,active,pending` splits on the leading `,` → `['active', 'pending']`. `?btw-year=-2020-2024` splits on `-` → `['2020', '2024']`. `btw` requires exactly two values.
- `parseSearch(input, {mode?, caseSensitive?, maxLength?})` → `{query, mode?, caseSensitive?} | null`. `maxLength` default `1024`.
- `parsePatch(body, {metaPrefix?})` → `{patch, options}` — splits wire body into raw patch + `{delete, separator, arrayOps}`. Patch accumulator is a null-prototype object (defends against `__proto__` in the request body).
- `parseNames(input, {maxItems?})` → `string[]`. `maxItems` default `1000`.
- `parsePaging(input, {defaultLimit?, maxLimit?, maxOffset?})` → `{offset, limit}` (clamped). `maxOffset` default `100_000` — caps `?offset=` to prevent DoS via `?offset=1e15` driving `paginateList` into astronomical skip-page SDK calls.
- `parseFlag(input)` → `boolean` — accepts `yes` / `true` / `1` / `on` (case-insensitive).
- `coerceStringQuery(query)` → `Record<string, string>` — filters framework query bag to string values only (drops nested objects / numbers; picks first string from arrays). Null-prototype accumulator defends against `?constructor=…` / `?__proto__=…` shadowing.

### 6.2 Builders

- `buildEnvelope(result, {keys?, links?})` → wraps `{data, offset, limit, total?}` with configurable key names; optionally embeds `links`.
- `buildErrorBody(err, {includeDebug?, errorId?})` → `{code, message, errorId?, stack?}`
- `paginationLinks(offset, limit, total, urlBuilder?)` → `{prev: string | null, next: string | null}` — `urlBuilder` is `({offset, limit}) => string`.
- `buildListOptions(query, policy)` → `ListOptionsBase` — composes `parseFields` / `parseSearch` / `parseFilter` / `parsePaging` / `parseFlag` under policy's pagination caps. Returns `{offset, limit, consistent, needTotal, fields?, filter?, search?}` ready for `adapter.getList`. Recognises `?fields=*keys` wildcard → `keysOnly: true`.
- `resolveSort(query, sortableIndices?)` → `{index, descending}` — resolves `?sort=-name` to `{index: sortableIndices.name, descending: true}`. Unmapped / missing fields → `{index: undefined, descending: false}`.
- `stripMount(pathname, mountPath?)` → `string | null` — returns the pathname tail when the request is under `mountPath`, else `null`. Normalizes trailing slash so `'/planets/'` and `'/planets'` behave identically. Empty / missing `mountPath` means "mount at root."
- `validateWriteBody(body, {allowEmpty?, allowArray?}?)` → `body` — throws `{status: 400, code: 'BadBody'}` when `body` is non-object (or null/array without the matching option). Use on write-shaped routes where `{...body, ...key}` would silently accept `null` (writes key-only) or arrays (writes `{"0": …}`).

### 6.3 Policy

```ts
const defaultPolicy = {
  metaPrefix: '_',                          // patch meta keys (_delete, _separator, _arrayOps)
  dbPrefix: '-',                            // DB-internal field prefix (informational)
  methodPrefix: '-',                        // URL method prefix (/-by-names, /-clone)
  envelope: {items: 'data', total: 'total', offset: 'offset', limit: 'limit', links: 'links'},
  statusCodes: {miss: 404, validation: 422, consistency: 409, throttle: 429, transient: 503, internal: 500},
  errorBody: buildErrorBody,
  needTotal: true,
  defaultLimit: 10,
  maxLimit: 100,
  maxOffset: 100_000
};
```

- `mapErrorStatus(err, statusCodes?)` → `number`. Recognised SDK names: `ConditionalCheckFailedException` / `TransactionCanceledException` / `TransactionConflictException` → `consistency`; `ValidationException` / `ValidationError` → `validation`; `ProvisionedThroughputExceededException` / `RequestLimitExceeded` → `throttle`; 5xx SDK → `transient`; everything else → `internal`. Toolkit errors with a `.status` property short-circuit (used by `validateWriteBody`'s `{status: 400, code: 'BadBody'}`).
- `mergePolicy(overrides)` → deep-merged `RestPolicy`. `envelope` and `statusCodes` merge key-by-key; everything else is shallow-merged.

### 6.4 ListOptionsBase

```ts
interface ListOptionsBase {
  offset: number;
  limit: number;
  consistent: boolean;
  needTotal: boolean;
  fields?: string | string[];
  keysOnly?: boolean;
  search?: string;
  caseSensitive?: boolean;
  filter?: FilterClause[];
}
```

### 6.5 Filter URL grammar (Option W)

Every query-param of shape `<op>-<field>=<value>` is a filter clause. `parseFilter` extracts them; `adapter.applyFilter` validates against `filterable` and emits expressions.

| op | Meaning | Arity |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `eq` | `=` | scalar |
| `ne` | `<>` | scalar |
| `lt` / `le` / `gt` / `ge` | `<` / `<=` / `>` / `>=` | scalar |
| `in` | `IN (...)` | array (first-char delimiter) |
| `btw` | `BETWEEN a AND b` | 2-array (first-char delimiter) |
| `beg` | `begins_with` | scalar |
| `ct` | `contains` | scalar |
| `ex` / `nx` | `attribute_exists` / `_not_exists` | none |

**Examples:**
- `?eq-status=active&eq-tier=gold`
- `?in-state=,TX,CA,NY` — first char `,` delimits
- `?btw-year=-2020-2024` — first char `-` delimits
- `?ex-email=` — value ignored
- Wire grammar doesn't need `?search=` — `?beg-name=A` / `?ct-name=sky` are fine too (but `?search=` runs over the pre-lowercased `searchable` mirrors which is cheaper for free-form text).

Clauses that match an index pk/sk auto-promote to `KeyConditionExpression` (single-clause per key component). The rest compile into `FilterExpression`. `BadFilterField` / `BadFilterOp` are thrown when the field isn't in `filterable` or the op isn't allowlisted.

## 7. Handler (`dynamodb-toolkit/handler`)

```js
import {createServer} from 'node:http';
import {createHandler} from 'dynamodb-toolkit/handler';

const handler = createHandler(adapter, {
  policy: {/* override defaults */},
  sortableIndices: {name: 'by-name-index'},
  keyFromPath: (rawKey, adapter) => ({[adapter.keyFields[0].name]: rawKey}),
  exampleFromContext: (query, body) => ({}),
  maxBodyBytes: 1024 * 1024
});

createServer(handler).listen(3000);
```

**Key invariants:**

- **HEAD → GET auto-promote.** `matchRoute` treats `HEAD` as `GET` for dispatch, annotates the result with `head: true`, and the bundled handler returns headers + `Content-Length` with an empty body.
- **Body-always-parsed.** For clone-all / move-all routes, the parsed body flows into `exampleFromContext(query, body)` alongside the query — consumers can key scope on either.
- **Body cap in bytes.** The built-in body reader accumulates `Buffer` chunks and enforces `maxBodyBytes` in bytes. Multi-byte payloads (CJK, emoji) are bounded at the advertised cap.
- **`readJsonBody(req, maxBodyBytes, {destroy?})`** — exported helper with streaming `TextDecoder` (~1× body size peak memory). `{destroy: false}` when the framework needs the socket alive to write the 413 response.

### 7.1 Standard route pack

| Method | URL | Adapter call |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `GET` | `/` | `getList` + envelope |
| `POST` | `/` | `post` (204) |
| `DELETE` | `/?<op>-f=…&search=…` | `deleteListByParams` (`MassOpResult`) |
| `GET` | `/-by-names?names=…&fields=…` | `getByKeys` (length-preserving; `null` at misses) |
| `DELETE` | `/-by-names?names=…` | `deleteByKeys` (`{processed}`) |
| `PUT` | `/-load` | `putItems` (body: array; `{processed}`) |
| `PUT` | `/-clone` / `/-move` | `cloneListByParams` / `moveListByParams` (body: overlay; `MassOpResult`) |
| `PUT` | `/-clone-by-names?names=…` / `/-move-by-names?names=…` | `cloneByKeys` / `moveByKeys` |
| `GET` | `/:key` | `getByKey` (404 on miss) |
| `PUT` | `/:key?force=true` | `put` (204) |
| `PATCH` | `/:key` | `patch` (body: `{field, _delete, _separator, _arrayOps}`) |
| `DELETE` | `/:key` | `delete` (204, idempotent) |
| `PUT` | `/:key/-clone` / `/:key/-move` | single-item `clone` / `move` |

Method prefix `-` is configurable via `policy.methodPrefix`. `keyFromPath` default uses `adapter.keyFields[0].name`.

### 7.2 Query parameters

- `?fields=name,climate` — projection. `?fields=*keys` → `keysOnlyFields`.
- `?search=tatooine` — free-form substring over `searchable` mirrors.
- `?<op>-<field>=<value>` — structured filter grammar (§6.5).
- `?sort=name` / `?sort=-name` — ascending / descending; resolved via `sortableIndices`.
- `?offset=N&limit=N` — pagination (clamped by `policy.maxOffset` / `policy.maxLimit`).
- `?consistent=yes|true|1|on` — strong consistency.
- `?force=yes|true|1|on` — overwrite on `put`/`clone`/`move`.

### 7.3 Error mapping

Per `policy.statusCodes`. Default: `ConditionalCheckFailedException` / `TransactionCanceledException` / `TransactionConflictException` → 409, `ValidationException` → 422, `ProvisionedThroughputExceededException` / `RequestLimitExceeded` → 429, 5xx SDK or `InternalServerError` → 503, anything else → 500. Toolkit errors carry `.status`; the handler respects that before running `mapErrorStatus`.

`policy.errorBody` builds the response body. Default returns `{code, message}`.

### 7.4 Framework adapters (separate packages)

The bundled handler is `node:http`-only. Framework bindings ship as separate unscoped npm packages so the core keeps zero runtime dependencies. Each is a thin wrapper (~150–300 LoC) that translates its framework's request/response shape into calls through the same `rest-core` parsers/builders + `matchRoute`. Wire contract is identical across all four.

| Package | Runtime / framework | Peer deps | Version |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `dynamodb-toolkit-koa` | Koa 2.x / 3.x | `koa` | 0.3.0 (peer `dynamodb-toolkit@^3.7.0`) |
| `dynamodb-toolkit-express` | Express 4.x / 5.x | `express` | 0.3.0 |
| `dynamodb-toolkit-fetch` | Fetch API — `(Request) => Promise<Response>`. Runs on Cloudflare Workers, Deno Deploy, Bun.serve, Hono, Node native fetch server | none | 0.3.0 |
| `dynamodb-toolkit-lambda` | AWS Lambda — API Gateway REST (v1), HTTP (v2), Function URL, ALB. Auto-detects event shape. Ships `createNodeListener` / `createFetchBridge` for local-debug without `sam local` | none | 0.3.0 |

## 8. Provisioning (`dynamodb-toolkit/provisioning`)

Separate submodule — not Adapter methods. IaC-managed deployments (Terraform / CDK / CloudFormation) pay no runtime cost on Adapter import.

### 8.1 API

```js
import {planTable, ensureTable, verifyTable, diffTable} from 'dynamodb-toolkit/provisioning';

// Read-only: what would ensureTable do?
const plan = await planTable(adapter);
// → {tableName, steps: PlanStep[], summary: string[]}

// Execute the plan.
const {plan, executed, descriptorWritten} = await ensureTable(adapter);
// → descriptorWritten: true when descriptorKey is declared and the snapshot was written

// Verify declaration matches live.
const {ok, diffs} = await verifyTable(adapter, {throwOnMismatch: true, requireDescriptor: false});
// → ok: false if any error-severity diff; diffs: TableDiff[]

// Pure diff — no I/O.
const diffs = diffTable(extractDeclaration(adapter), liveDescribeTableOutput);
```

**`PlanStep`:**

```ts
type PlanStep =
  | {action: 'create'; params: Record<string, unknown>}
  | {action: 'add-gsi'; name: string; params: Record<string, unknown>}
  | {action: 'skip-extra-gsi'; name: string}
  | {action: 'skip-extra-lsi'; name: string}
  | {action: 'skip-missing-lsi'; name: string};
```

**`TableDiff`:**

```ts
interface TableDiff {
  path: string;        // e.g. 'table.KeySchema', 'gsi.by-foo', 'descriptor.indices'
  severity: 'error' | 'warn';   // error: declared-vs-actual mismatch; warn: extras or descriptor drift
  expected: unknown;
  actual: unknown;
}
```

**Policy:** toolkit owns the plan (ADD-only, never destructive); DynamoDB owns legality (LSI-on-existing-table attempts, key-schema changes, etc.) — those surface as SDK rejections unchanged. No pre-checks that could drift from AWS's evolving rules.

### 8.2 Descriptor record

Opt-in via `descriptorKey` on the Adapter (e.g., `'__adapter__'`). The provisioning helpers write a JSON snapshot of the Adapter's declaration at `{[keyFields[0]]: descriptorKey}` — captures what `DescribeTable` can't see:

- Marshalling-field names + types
- `searchable` map + `searchablePrefix`
- `filterable` allowlist + types
- `versionField` / `createdAtField` names
- `typeField` / `typeDiscriminator` / `typeLabels`

Absence is **neutral by default** (IaC-managed tables don't need the descriptor). `{requireDescriptor: true}` on `verifyTable` opts into strict "toolkit-managed" assertion.

Helpers: `buildDescriptorSnapshot(declaration)` → snapshot object; `compareDescriptor(expected, actual)` → diffs; `readDescriptor(client, table, descriptorKey)` / `writeDescriptor(client, table, descriptorKey, snapshot)` — direct access. `DESCRIPTOR_VERSION` — current snapshot schema version.

### 8.3 CLI

```sh
npx dynamodb-toolkit plan-table ./adapter.js
npx dynamodb-toolkit ensure-table ./adapter.js [--json]
npx dynamodb-toolkit verify-table ./adapter.js [--strict] [--require-descriptor] [--json]
```

The `<module>` is ESM-dynamic-imported. Exports one of:
- `export default adapter;`
- `export const adapter = new Adapter(...);`
- `export async function buildAdapter() { ... }` (CLI will await it)

Flags:
- `--strict` on `verify-table` — exit 1 on any error-severity diff.
- `--require-descriptor` on `verify-table` — exit 1 when `descriptorKey` is declared but the row is missing.
- `--json` on any — machine-readable output (the result object) instead of human summary.

Exit codes: `0` clean, `1` diffs or error.

## 9. Marshalling (`dynamodb-toolkit/marshalling`)

Symmetric marshaller pairs. Packaging both directions in a single object nudges callers to keep write + read paths in sync.

```ts
interface Marshaller<TRuntime, TStored> {
  marshall: (value: TRuntime) => TStored;
  unmarshall: (stored: TStored) => TRuntime;
}
```

- `marshallDateISO(date)` / `unmarshallDateISO(iso)` — `Date` ⇔ `string` (ISO 8601). `undefined` / `null` pass through.
- `marshallDateEpoch(date)` / `unmarshallDateEpoch(ms)` — `Date` ⇔ `number` (epoch ms). Pass-through nulls.
- `dateISO`, `dateEpoch` — `Marshaller<Date, string>` / `Marshaller<Date, number>` convenience pairs.
- `marshallMap(map, valueTransform?)` / `unmarshallMap(obj)` — `Map<string, V>` ⇔ plain object. Optional per-value transform.
- `marshallURL(url)` / `unmarshallURL(str)` — `URL` ⇔ `string`.
- `url` — `Marshaller<URL, string>`.

Typical usage in hooks:

```js
import {dateISO, marshallMap, unmarshallMap} from 'dynamodb-toolkit/marshalling';

hooks: {
  prepare(item) {
    return {
      ...item,
      createdAt: dateISO.marshall(item.createdAt),
      tags: marshallMap(item.tags)
    };
  },
  revive(raw) {
    return {
      ...raw,
      createdAt: dateISO.unmarshall(raw.createdAt),
      tags: unmarshallMap(raw.tags)
    };
  }
}
```

## 10. Hook builders (`dynamodb-toolkit/hooks`)

Re-exported from the root.

- `stampCreatedAtISO(fieldName = '_createdAt')` → `prepare` hook that writes `new Date().toISOString()` on first insert only. Items carrying the field (round-tripped from reads) are untouched; patches are untouched.
- `stampCreatedAtEpoch(fieldName = '_createdAt')` → same but writes `Date.now()`.

Generic in item shape; compose via `stampCreatedAtISO<TItem>('-createdAt')` for typed adapters.

## 11. Optimistic concurrency (`versionField`)

```js
new Adapter({
  client, table, keyFields: ['id'],
  technicalPrefix: '-',
  versionField: '-version'
});
```

**Write-path semantics:**

- `post` — inits `versionField = 1`. Writes with `attribute_not_exists(<pk>)`.
- `put(item)` — reads the field from the caller's item. Conditions on `attribute_not_exists(<pk>) OR <versionField> = :observed`. Writes `observed + 1` (or `1` if new). `{force: true}` bypasses the condition but still bumps the version.
- `patch(key, patch, {expectedVersion?})` — conditions on `attribute_not_exists(<pk>) OR <versionField> = :expectedVersion` when supplied; falls through to `attribute_exists(<pk>)` otherwise. UpdateExpression always `ADD <versionField> :1` when declared.
- `delete(key, {expectedVersion?})` — conditions on `<versionField> = :expectedVersion` when supplied; no increment (item is gone).
- `edit(key, mapFn)` / `editListByParams(params, mapFn)` — read the observed version, condition on it, increment. Mass-op results bucket CCF-on-version-mismatch into `conflicts: [{key, reason: 'VersionConflict', sdkError}]`.

Round-trips: `revive` preserves `versionField` + `createdAtField` (unlike other `technicalPrefix`-stripped fields) so read-modify-write cycles work without explicit handling.

## 12. Scope-freeze (`createdAtField` + `asOf`)

```js
new Adapter({client, table, keyFields: ['id'], technicalPrefix: '-', createdAtField: '-createdAt'});

// Replay scope — only items that existed at or before the cutoff
await adapter.deleteListByParams(params, {asOf: '2026-04-01T00:00:00Z'});
```

`options.asOf` AND-merges `<createdAtField> <= :asOf` into the `Query`/`Scan` `FilterExpression`. Throws `CreatedAtFieldNotDeclared` when `createdAtField` isn't set.

The toolkit does NOT auto-write `createdAtField`. Use `stampCreatedAtISO` / `stampCreatedAtEpoch` in the user's `prepare` hook. The stored format dictates what `asOf` values to pass. `Date` is auto-converted to ISO 8601; other types pass through untouched.

## 13. Error classes

All extend `ToolkitError` (which extends `Error`). `err.name` matches the class name for `name`-based discrimination. Toolkit errors with `.status` short-circuit the handler's default error mapping.

```ts
class ConsistentReadOnGSIRejected extends ToolkitError { indexName: string; }
class NoIndexForSortField extends ToolkitError { sortField: string; }
class BadFilterField extends ToolkitError { field: string; }
class BadFilterOp extends ToolkitError { field: string; op: string; }
class KeyFieldChanged extends ToolkitError { fields: string[]; }
class CreatedAtFieldNotDeclared extends ToolkitError { }
class CascadeNotDeclared extends ToolkitError { operation: string; }
class TableVerificationFailed extends ToolkitError {
  tableName: string;
  diffs: Array<{path, severity, expected, actual}>;
}
class TransactionLimitExceededError extends ToolkitError { count: number; limit: number; }
```

## 14. Bare helpers

- `import {sleep} from 'dynamodb-toolkit'` — `sleep(ms): Promise<void>`.
- `import {seq} from 'dynamodb-toolkit'` — sequential async runner: `seq([fn1, fn2, ...])` awaits each in order.
- `import {random} from 'dynamodb-toolkit'` — `random(length = 8)`. Crypto-random lowercase alphanumeric string. Not cryptographically strong for secrets — unique-enough tag (test table names, scratch keys, idempotency tokens).

## 15. Testing recipes

**Unit tests against a mocked client:**

```js
import {mock} from 'node:test';
import {Adapter, raw} from 'dynamodb-toolkit';

const send = mock.fn(async cmd => {
  if (cmd.constructor.name === 'GetCommand') return {Item: {name: 'X'}};
  return {};
});
const adapter = new Adapter({client: {send}, table: 'T', keyFields: ['name']});
```

**End-to-end against DynamoDB Local:**

See `tests/helpers/dynamodb-local.js` — spawns `amazon/dynamodb-local` via Docker on a random port, skips with a clear message when Docker is unavailable.

## 16. Problem-first recipes

Pattern-first pages indexed at https://github.com/uhop/dynamodb-toolkit/wiki/Recipes. Each recipe opens with the SQL-equivalent question, shows the declaration + worked code, and calls out "when this doesn't fit." The full catalog (with one-line hooks) is in `llms.txt` §Recipes and on the wiki index page. Eleven recipes grouped as: **hierarchical data + type dispatch** (tier listing across / within partitions; per-tier sparse GSI markers) · **subtree operations** (`buildKey` shapes, cascade delete / clone / move) · **concurrency + time-bounded state** (reservation with auto-release) · **index design** (keys-only GSI with runtime projection) · **REST query surface** (Filter URL grammar, text search) · **bulk operations + lifecycle** (resumable mass operations, provisioning workflow).

Reach for a recipe when the question is "how do I do X" rather than "what does method Y do." For method-level detail stay in this file or `llms.txt`.

## 17. Migration v2 → v3

- AWS SDK v3 peer-deps replace `aws-sdk` v2.
- One data format (plain JS via `lib-dynamodb`); `Raw` / `DbRaw` collapsed to `Raw<T>`.
- Options bags everywhere — `put(item, {force: true})`, `patch(key, patch, {delete: [...]})`.
- Hooks renamed: `prepareListParams` → `prepareListInput`, `updateParams` → `updateInput`.
- Patch wire format: `_delete` / `_separator` (single underscore by default; configurable via `policy.metaPrefix`).
- REST split into `rest-core` (framework-agnostic) + `handler` (`node:http`). Koa lives in a separate package.
- Dropped: `makeClient`, `getProfileName`, `specialTypes`, `converter`, `converterOptions`. Use `@aws-sdk/credential-providers` (`fromIni`, `fromNodeProviderChain`) directly.
- `generic*` mass methods folded into `options.strategy: 'native' | 'sequential'`.
- Transaction limit raised 25 → 100.
- v2 documentation snapshot lives in the wiki at the `v2.3-docs` git tag. v2 source remains available on npm as `dynamodb-toolkit@2.3.0`.

## 18. Major 3.x milestones

- **3.1.1** — Pre-adapter security audit (prototype pollution closed in `paths/`, `parse-patch`, `read-ordered-list-by-keys`; DoS-on-self closed in batch retry loop, handler unbounded body, `?offset=`; silent-data-loss fixed in `moveList`, `moveByKeys`).
- **3.1.2** — Tier-A rest-core extractions (`buildListOptions`, `resolveSort`, `stripMount`, `coerceStringQuery`, `validateWriteBody`, `readJsonBody`). Byte-accurate body cap (was UTF-16 code units).
- **3.2.0** — Declarative schema (`structuralKey`, `indices`, `typeLabels`, `typeDiscriminator`, `filterable`, `technicalPrefix`), filter URL grammar (`f-<field>-<op>=` at the time), `readByKeys` / `writeItems` / `deleteByKeys` / `deleteListByParams` / `cloneListByParams` / `moveListByParams` renames, D2 length-preserving `getByKeys`.
- **3.3.0** — Resumable mass ops (cursor + `MassOpOptions` / `MassOpResult`), `edit(key, mapFn)`, `editListByParams`, `rename`, `cloneWithOverwrite`, GIGO cleanup (no runtime type-checking of TS-typed args).
- **3.4.0** — `versionField` (optimistic concurrency), `createdAtField` + `asOf` (scope-freeze), `/marshalling` subpath.
- **3.5.0** — Cascade (`relationships.structural` + `deleteAllUnder` / `cloneAllUnder{,By}` / `moveAllUnder{,By}`).
- **3.6.0** — `/provisioning` subpath (`planTable` / `ensureTable` / `verifyTable`, descriptor record, CLI).
- **3.6.1** — Ergonomics-review bug-fix patch (`buildKey` composite pk, `patch` + `versionField` conditions, `getListByParams` honours options).
- **3.7.0** — Filter URL grammar flipped to **Option W** (`?<op>-<field>=<value>`; the 3.2-era `?f-<field>-<op>=` form is removed), `options.filter` / `options.search` split, `planTable` / `ensureTable` split (no more `{yes}` / `{dryRun}`), `buildKey` simplification (children default, `{self: true}`, `{partial: 'abc'}`, `kind: 'exact'` dropped), `typeField` auto-populate, `stampCreatedAtISO` / `stampCreatedAtEpoch` hook builders, `getListUnder` sugar, `filterable: {ops, type}` form, descriptor-hidden-from-scans default. Break-in-minor with no compat shims except the soft-warned method aliases (`getAll`, `putAll`, …).

## Links

- Wiki: https://github.com/uhop/dynamodb-toolkit/wiki
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/dynamodb-toolkit
- Short reference: https://github.com/uhop/dynamodb-toolkit/blob/master/llms.txt
